target.name: H2Pro
target.founder: Gideon Grader, Avner Rothschild, Hen Dotan, Talmon Marco

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
H2Pro, a Series B–stage climate and energy company that has raised $100M+, is evaluated against a universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public entity and 8 private firms operating in the same sector. The peer set median valuation is $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B; Commonwealth Fusion Systems represents the high outlier at $5.0B, while the median peer cluster (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.) clusters between $300M and $500M.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses H2Pro's $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B progression, implying a $190M valuation. The peer-median method directly benchmarks H2Pro against the six same-stage, same-sector comparables, yielding a $355M implied valuation. These methods reflect substantially different risk and capital intensity assumptions embedded in peer valuations.

The triangulated analysis synthesizes these approaches into a range: $162M (low), $272M (median), and $408M (high). The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around H2Pro's capital requirements, time to commercialization, and competitive positioning within a heterogeneous peer set. The median estimate of $272M sits between the stage-based and peer-median anchors, acknowledging both historical funding patterns and contemporaneous market comparables. Key sensitivities include technological maturation risk and the sustainability of climate-tech funding appetites.
